intro: Mascagnia lucida is a species of flowering plant in the family Malpighiaceae, commonly known as the "Lucid Mascagnia" due to its shiny, leathery leaves. The plant is traditionally used in folk medicine, and studies have shown that it exhibits various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which can help to alleviate symptoms of arthritis, reduce oxidative stress, and prevent infections. The bioactive constituents of Mascagnia lucida include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are commonly used to treat fever, cough, and respiratory issues.
